clean_body: We’re not going to be offering these cards for much longer, and President Biden and Vice President Harris are counting on grassroots supporters to renew their membership before tomorrow’s deadline. | | Hi Derek, I’m taking a quick break from putting together our next shipment of 2023 DNC Member Cards, and I wanted to connect with you to see if you’ll I’ve heard from a ton of folks who have already received their cards and they couldn’t be more thrilled. We’re not going to be offering these cards for much longer, and President Biden and Vice President Harris are counting on grassroots supporters to renew their membership and help fund critical programs that helped win elections nationwide last year. I can’t emphasize enough how much grassroots donations as little as $10 are making a difference -- especially this early in the election cycle. We need your help because there’s too much at stake. I’m excited to see your donation come through and to get your card ready for you! name: Peter, trying to reach you (via moms in office)
subject: Time is running out
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY MOMS IN OFFICE
body:
https://www.everyaction.com/ [https://www.everyaction.com/]
Hey there,
Peter, Moms in Office is working every single day to elect women who will pass policies that work for working families, not corporations and big donors. Every grassroots contribution helps to power our work and grow our movement.
Our February fundraising deadline is tomorrow at midnight and we need 27 people from your area to chip in if we want to reach our $10,000 fundraising goal. Can we count on you to be one of the 27 people to get us closer to our goal? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/momsinoffice]
As a year-round, grassroots movement, the donations we receive today will help us:
✔Ensure Moms in Office can continue to push for policy solutions that families long deserved
✔Fund more candidates on the state level in critical states where our reproductive rights are under assault
✔Pay our staff a living wage
✔Invest in outreach and organizing efforts to build our movement
It's critical that we reach every single fundraising goal that we set this year and right now we are running out of time to reach our goal of raising $10,000 before midnight tomorrow.
Can you chip in $5, or any amount you can, to make sure we reach our February fundraising goal before midnight tomorrow? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/momsinoffice]
With your help, we have accomplished so much in 2022! Now is the time to get ready for 2024!
https://www.gov.ca.gov/2023/01/31/big-oil-made-record-2022-profits-while-fleecing-california-families/ [https://www.gov.ca.gov/2023/01/31/big-oil-made-record-2022-profits-while-fleecing-california-families/]
CHIP IN [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/momsinoffice]

P.S. The good news is, our finance team let us know that we’re getting close to reaching our fundraising goal — this means your support today is more important now than ever.
Thank you for everything!
Simona Grace
Founder and Executive Director
Moms in Office

name: Elissa Slotkin
subject: One last ask today for a strong launch
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ELISSA SLOTKIN FOR MICHIGAN
body:
# [](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/go/188?t=1&akid=24%2E135344%2EYW3X9R)
Friend -
Well, it’s been quite a day over here on Team Slotkin, where we find ourselves in the final hours of our launch day! The support for our campaign from Michigan and across the country has been great.
But of course, not everyone is as happy as we are. We know that our potential opponents on the other side of the aisle are watching to see how strong our launch is, and to put it bluntly: how much money we’re able to raise today, as a signal of support.
They know that the amount of money a candidate can raise in the early days is a good indicator of how strong a campaign they’re prepared to run.
They also know that in campaigns, money in the bank means the ability to communicate with more voters, getting our message out sooner and to more people. For example - your support is helping us to kick off a listening tour through Michigan, starting with Detroit later this week, to hear what people throughout the state want from their leaders.
But separate from what the other side thinks, one thing is very clear right now: Washington is in desperate, desperate need of normal, pragmatic, common-sense leadership.
My job today is to make sure that we have the strongest possible launch, so we can win, keep this seat in a notoriously swingy state, and send someone to the Senate who will work to get things done for Michigan and the country.
So that’s why I’m back, asking at this late hour for as many donations as possible before the end of the day. We have a few hours left to make more progress, which is why I’m back haunting your inbox:
[Will you make your first contribution to my Senate campaign? When regular, normal people chip in what they can, when they can, we’re well on our way to winning this race.**](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/go/9?c0=ebs-em-febu23.o9qbqY&refcode=em230227-3-15&t=2&akid=24%2E135344%2EYW3X9R)

clean_body: Friend - Well, it’s been quite a day over here on Team Slotkin, where we find ourselves in the final hours of our launch day! The support for our campaign from Michigan and across the country has been great. But of course, not everyone is as happy as we are. We know that our potential opponents on the other side of the aisle are watching to see how strong our launch is, and to put it bluntly: how much money we’re able to raise today, as a signal of support. They know that the amount of money a candidate can raise in the early days is a good indicator of how strong a campaign they’re prepared to run. They also know that in campaigns, money in the bank means the ability to communicate with more voters, getting our message out sooner and to more people. For example - your support is helping us to kick off a listening tour through Michigan, starting with Detroit later this week, to hear what people throughout the state want from their leaders. But separate from what the other side thinks, one thing is very clear right now: Washington is in desperate, desperate need of normal, pragmatic, common-sense leadership. My job today is to make sure that we have the strongest possible launch, so we can win, keep this seat in a notoriously swingy state, and send someone to the Senate who will work to get things done for Michigan and the country. So that’s why I’m back, asking at this late hour for as many donations as possible before the end of the day. We have a few hours left to make more progress, which is why I’m back haunting your inbox: If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly: | | ** | | | | | | | ** Thank you for ALL of your support today, Elissa PAID FOR BY ELISSA SLOTKIN FOR MICHIGAN P.O. Box 4145 East Lansing, MI 48826

name: Adam Schiff
subject: This won’t take long
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY SCHIFF FOR SENATE
body:
[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/62?t=1001&akid=1861%2E5257348%2EmtjXc1 ]Adam Schiff for Senate

Derek — it’s Adam.

We’re just hours away from our end-of-month fundraising deadline, so I
hope you’ll give me a chance to explain why this month’s goal is so
important.

I’m writing to you from the car right now, where we’re currently finishing
up our first California for All tour around our great state.

Since launching our campaign for U.S. Senate last month, our team has
worked nonstop to build the resources and campaign to reach every single
voter in our nation’s largest state. (That’s more than 21 million voters!)

Our work is already paying off: A new poll from the LA Times has our
campaign slightly in the lead — with many more than 40% of the electorate
still up for grabs.

We’re building a movement we can all be proud of. Our campaign doesn’t
accept a dime of corporate PAC money — making it all the more important we
can grow our grassroots digital fundraising. This is our first full month
since jumping in the race, so it’s important we start off our campaign on
the right foot.

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1014?c0=feb2023_abs_em.Rua6IW&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_EOM_AC_20230227_PM1_V1_S1&email=&t=1004&akid=1861%2E5257348%2EmtjXc1 ]We need 46 more donations in these final 24 hours to hit our
fundraising goal — so that we can continue to travel the Golden State and
build a grassroots movement to defend our democracy. Can you help us grow
our fundraising numbers in Cresco by making a donation now?

If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your
donation will go through immediately:

| Friend, this past week, Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene effectively called for the nation to split in two, divided between “red” states and “blue” states.

Look, I want to be unequivocally clear: **That is a _horrible_ idea.**

This is the type of hyper partisanship you’ll only find from politicians in Washington looking to score the cheapest of political points.

[**It’s up to all of us to call this type of behavior out for what it is: hateful politicking for personal gain. name: =?utf-8?Q?Dave=20Min=20for=20Congress?=
subject: RELEASE: California State Superintendent Of Public Instruction Tony Thurmond Endorses Dave Min for Congress
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dave Min for Congress 2024
body:
Former UC Irvine Law Professor Min Has Been A Champion For Students, Teachers, And Safe Classrooms

View this email in your browser (https://mailchi.mp/1a839e568270/releasecalifornia-state-superintendent-of-public-instruction-tony-thurmond-endorses-dave-min-for-congress-15650691?e=7017cc100e)

For Immediate Release: February 27, 2023
Contact: press@davemin.com (mailto:press@davemin.com)

California State Superintendent Of Public Instruction Tony Thurmond Endorses Dave Min for Congress

Former UC Irvine Law Professor Min, A Proud Product Of California Public Schools, Whose Children Attend Public Schools In The District, Has Been A Champion For Students, Teachers, And Safe Classrooms

IRVINE, CA — Today, California State Superintendent of Public Instruction Tony Thurmond endorsed Dave Min in his campaign for California’s 47th Congressional District. Dave Min currently serves in the California State Senate and was formerly a law professor at the University of California, Irvine School of Law. Senator Min, who was elected in 2020, is the child of Korean immigrants and was the proud product of California public schools, which he attended from kindergarten through high school. He currently has three young children in the Irvine Unified School District, which is the largest school district in CA-47. Because of Dave’s lived experience of California public education being a pathway to economic opportunity, Dave has been a champion for our public schools in the State Senate, including serving as one of only four members of the Budget Subcommittee on Education, which oversees funding for K-12 schools, community colleges, the California State University system, and the University
of California.

“I’m proud to support Dave Min in his campaign for the 47th Congressional District,” said California Superintendent of Public Instruction Tony Thurmond. “As a father of three young kids in public school, who was himself the product of California public schools and a former UC Irvine educator, Dave is uniquely equipped to understand both the importance of education in achieving the American Dream and also the challenges that we face in trying to promote high quality public education. Whether it’s his work to protect our schools from gun violence or his efforts to expand educational opportunity, Dave has fought tirelessly for students, families, and teachers throughout his career. We need his voice in Congress.”

Tony Thurmond is the twenty-eighth California State Superintendent of Public Instruction overseeing California’s Department of Education and more than seven million children and young adults in more than 9,000 schools. The CDE and the State Superintendent of Public Instruction are responsible for enforcing education law and regulations; and for continuing to reform and improve public elementary school programs, secondary school programs, adult education, some preschool programs, and child care programs. Thurmond, a former state Assemblyman, and school board member, has overseen historic investments to return to safe in-person instruction, the expansion of universal school meals, universal prekindergarten, and mental health support to both families, and teachers.

On the State Senate Budget Subcommittee on Education, Senator Min has expanded early childhood education, tackled food insecurity among school children, and launched innovative programs to address COVID-related learning loss. Min has has authored 16 bills signed into law by the Governor, including SB 312, which expands access to higher education for foster youth, and SB 1299, which invested in programs designed to expand opportunities in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM) to middle and high school students. Min is also the author of critical gun safety (https://www.latimes.com/socal/daily-pilot/news/story/2022-07-25/bill-aiming-to-ban-gun-shows-sales-on-state-land-hits-target) legislation as schools and communities across the country are savaged by gun violence.

Min’s Congressional campaign has been endorsed by 17 school board and community college board trustees in the district. Min was previously endorsed in his 2020 campaign for State Senate by the California Teachers Association, the California Federation of Teachers, the California School Employees Association, and the California Faculty Association, among others.

The only Korea-American in the legislature, Min serves as Chair of the Senate Natural Resources and Water Committee and as Senate Vice Chair of the Asian and Pacific Islander Legislative Caucus. He previously chaired the Senate Cybersecurity Select Committee. Before entering the State Senate, Min was a law professor at UC Irvine, where he taught and researched in the area of business law. He resides with his wife Jane, a UC Irvine law professor who specializes in domestic violence and family law, and their three children in Irvine. Dave is a native Californian, a product of California public schools who graduated from the Wharton School and Harvard Law School, and an Eagle Scout.

############

California’s 47th is an open Congressional District in Orange County that includes the Cities of Costa Mesa, Huntington Beach, Irvine, Newport Beach, and Seal Beach, and portions of Laguna Beach, Laguna Hills, and Laguna Woods. Incumbent Katie Porter is vacating the seat to run for U.S. Senate. In 2022, Porter defeated former Assemblymember Republican Scott Baugh by 3.44% in this “toss-up” district. State Senator Dave Min currently represents over 80% of Congressional District 47 in Sacramento. In 2020, Min received over 158,000 votes within CA-47, defeating incumbent John Moorlach by 4.3%. 19% of the registered voters in CA-47 are of Asian and Pacific Islander descent, including one of the largest Korean-American populations in the country.

============================================================

Irvine, CA 92616

That's why it’s _absolutely crucial_ that we meet tomorrow’s midnight goal, Peter — it’s the only way to keep up our critical work to defend our Democracy.

name: Jon Tester
subject: I first ran for office because I didn’t see anyone sticking up for dirt farmers like me
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hala for State Senate
body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59378185/393995327/1741422039?refcode=EM_FR_230227JT_N_JT_Actives_10_G_N_P1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODk2MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2M2MzZGY3Ni1mOGI2LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=rZNT5_IsLE6s_E9wCwJDLdn6YYgvt4eDSC9IbSZjfwA=) Peter, I have an exciting announcement and I want you to be among the first to know: **I’m officially running for reelection here in Montana.**

I know this race won’t be easy, but my fight for working families isn’t done yet.

[**If you’re with me, please split a donation between Hala Ayala and my campaign right now to become a Founding Donor. subject: In 2023, ballot measures are the new battleground for reproductive rights
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Fairness Project The Fairness Project
body:
In several states last year, voters decisively chose to protect abortion rights at the ballot box. But now, anti-choice legislators in other states are trying to silence voters by making it harder to pass ballot measures altogether. | [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/59378294/393995714/765663662?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRlAvMS84NTQ3OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyYzkxYTkxZS1mOWI2LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=MTMUkF2U69CXY36DiXxrES8P82rzZyc9XeusaXcxJ8o=&emci=3255c09f-eab6-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=2c91a91e-f9b6-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=561582) | Friends, the role of ballot measures in the fight for reproductive freedom has been getting more and more attention recently: [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/59378295/393995715/1580223838?refcode=E20416-20230227-EOM2-graphic-DeadlineEOM-RegTemplate-AbortionReproductiveRights-NA&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRlAvMS84NTQ3OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyYzkxYTkxZS1mOWI2LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=MTMUkF2U69CXY36DiXxrES8P82rzZyc9XeusaXcxJ8o=) The reason is simple: **Ballot measures have officially become the new battleground for abortion rights in 2023.[_Help us fight back_ → Rush an emergency donation before tomorrow’s End-of-Month deadline to our Ballot Measure Rescue Campaign (BMRC) now to protect ballot measures AND abortion rights in the states.](https://click.everyaction.com/k/59378296/393995716/1580223838?refcode=E20416-20230227-EOM2-SL-DeadlineEOM-RegTemplate-AbortionReproductiveRights-NA&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRlAvMS84NTQ3OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyYzkxYTkxZS1mOWI2LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=MTMUkF2U69CXY36DiXxrES8P82rzZyc9XeusaXcxJ8o=)** Last year, after _Roe v. Wade_ was struck down, anti-choice extremists pounced at the opportunity to reinstate 100-year-old trigger bans on the books from before the landmark ruling. **But when Election Day came around, voters fought back**. _All across the country, Americans stepped up at the ballot box to show these extremists that we will NOT back down in the fight to maintain our fundamental rights._ In several states last year, voters decisively chose to protect abortion rights at the ballot box. But now, anti-choice legislators in other states are trying to silence voters by making it harder to pass ballot measures altogether. **That’s why we’re gearing up to fight even harder to defend the ballot in 2023.** [HELP US FIGHT BACK!](https://click.everyaction.com/k/59378297/393995717/1580223838?refcode=E20416-20230227-EOM2-SB-DeadlineEOM-RegTemplate-AbortionReproductiveRights-NA&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRlAvMS84NTQ3OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyYzkxYTkxZS1mOWI2LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=MTMUkF2U69CXY36DiXxrES8P82rzZyc9XeusaXcxJ8o=) [**Our Ballot Measure Rescue Campaign is a $5 million effort to protect direct democracy from attacks on the ballot measure process. As more and more extremist legislators attempt to curtail the power of ballot measures, it’s critical that we keep the BMRC well-funded so we can stop any threats dead in their tracks. Will you chip in a donation before tomorrow’s fundraising deadline to help us hit our $10,000 goal and fuel our BMRC as we fight back against attacks on the ballot box?**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/59378298/393995718/1580223838?refcode=E20416-20230227-EOM2-SL-DeadlineEOM-RegTemplate-AbortionReproductiveRights-NA&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRlAvMS84NTQ3OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyYzkxYTkxZS1mOWI2LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=MTMUkF2U69CXY36DiXxrES8P82rzZyc9XeusaXcxJ8o=) | _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation to the Fairness Project will go through immediately:_ | **[$15](https://click.everyaction.com/k/59378299/393995719/1580223838?express_lane=true&refcode=E20416-20230227-EOM2-EL-DeadlineEOM-RegTemplate-AbortionReproductiveRights-NA&amount=15&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRlAvMS84NTQ3OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyYzkxYTkxZS1mOWI2LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=MTMUkF2U69CXY36DiXxrES8P82rzZyc9XeusaXcxJ8o=)** | **[$25](https://click.everyaction.com/k/59378300/393995720/1580223838?express_lane=true&refcode=E20416-20230227-EOM2-EL-DeadlineEOM-RegTemplate-AbortionReproductiveRights-NA&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRlAvMS84NTQ3OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyYzkxYTkxZS1mOWI2LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=MTMUkF2U69CXY36DiXxrES8P82rzZyc9XeusaXcxJ8o=)**

clean_body: In several states last year, voters decisively chose to protect abortion rights at the ballot box. But now, anti-choice legislators in other states are trying to silence voters by making it harder to pass ballot measures altogether. | | Friends, the role of ballot measures in the fight for reproductive freedom has been getting more and more attention recently: = The reason is simple: Ballot measures have officially become the new battleground for abortion rights in 2023.= Last year, after Roe v. Wade was struck down, anti-choice extremists pounced at the opportunity to reinstate 100-year-old trigger bans on the books from before the landmark ruling. But when Election Day came around, voters fought back. All across the country, Americans stepped up at the ballot box to show these extremists that we will NOT back down in the fight to maintain our fundamental rights. In several states last year, voters decisively chose to protect abortion rights at the ballot box. But now, anti-choice legislators in other states are trying to silence voters by making it harder to pass ballot measures altogether. That’s why we’re gearing up to fight even harder to defend the ballot in 2023. = = | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation to the Fairness Project will go through immediately: | = | = ---|--- = | = = | = Thank you, Team Fairness Project Thanks to grassroots supporters like you, our team at the Fairness Project has won 31 people-powered ballot measure campaigns since 2016, changing over 18 million lives by expanding health care, raising wages, guaranteeing paid leave, and ending predatory lending directly at the ballot box when politicians refuse to act. Your support allows us to make this progress possible.** = = Contributions or gifts to the Fairness Project are not deductible as charitable contributions for federal income tax purposes. Contributions to the Fairness Project support its many efforts nationwide and any focus is at the Fairness Project’s sole discretion. name: CaucusRoom
subject: The Blueprint to Ruin America
body:
You just need to work more. No images? [Click here](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-e-qkljrkd-trhiujldw-yh/) [](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-qkljrkd-trhiujldw-r/)
## **Happy Monday! The CaucusRoom Council[has a proposal posted for public comment.](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-qkljrkd-trhiujldw-y/) You can watch the meeting where this was discussed [here](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-qkljrkd-trhiujldw-j/) _._**
## **Here are some recent articles shared by CaucusRoom members. **
[](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-qkljrkd-trhiujldw-t/) [Schedule a CaucusRoom Live demo today!](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-qkljrkd-trhiujldw-i/) **Pentagon Lost Military Equipment** The Department of Defense, one of the largest federal agencies, **[has admitted to losing track of over $220 billion worth of equipment](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-qkljrkd-trhiujldw-d/)** it gave out to military contractors. **EPA Screws Up Again** Officials from Texas and Michigan**[say they were unaware that soil and water from the site of the East Palestine, Ohio](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-qkljrkd-trhiujldw-h/), **freight train derailment was being taken to their areas. **Stop the Insanity** Biden's new efficiency rule **[would effectively ban gas appliances](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-qkljrkd-trhiujldw-k/)** and any future gas products. 95% of the market would not meet the proposed rules. It turns out **[a dark money group would benefit.](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-qkljrkd-trhiujldw-u/) ** **Woody Harrelson Speaks the Truth** [**Woody Harrelson 's opening monologue during "Saturday Night Live," where he referenced the COVID-19 pandemic**](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-qkljrkd-trhiujldw-o/) and collaboration between the medical industry and the government to push vaccines, has sparked backlash online. Here is **[how the media reported it](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-qkljrkd-trhiujldw-x/)**. **Just Work More** A UK politician has advice for everyone dealing with the high food prices during this skyrocketing inflation. **[You all need to work more!](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-qkljrkd-trhiujldw-m/)** **Blueprint to Ruin America** Senator Tim Scott says the[ **Democrats are working on a blueprint to ruin America**](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-qkljrkd-trhiujldw-c/). It sure seems like many of Biden's decisions don't make any sense. **Everything is Fine**
## The number of migrants caught illegally crossing the southern border ['**has already hit ONE MILLION less than four months into the fiscal year,** '](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-qkljrkd-trhiujldw-q/) and is on track to smash records.

**Let****'s[ have a good laugh](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-qkljrkd-trhiujldw-a/)!**
* The Constitution [**doesn't mention crybabies**.](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-qkljrkd-trhiujldw-f/)
* **[He is the best](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-qkljrkd-trhiujldw-z/)**.
* It's just a **[costume](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-qkljrkd-trhiujldw-v/).**
* You know he's a**[train wreck!](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-qkljrkd-trhiujldw-e/)**

[](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-qkljrkd-trhiujldw-s/)[](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-qkljrkd-trhiujldw-g/)[](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-qkljrkd-trhiujldw-w/) [](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-qkljrkd-trhiujldw-yd/) | CaucusRoom, LLC
800 Washington Ave Ste C
Golden, CO 80401 You are receiving this email because you are a valued member of CaucusRoom [Preferences](https://caucusroom.updatemyprofile.com/i-qkljrkd-6F882DBF-trhiujldw-yk) | [Unsubscribe](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-u-qkljrkd-trhiujldw-yu/)
